checks.a_analysis: The output body text, divided into several large paragraph elements, faithfully represents the meeting minutes found on pages 3 through 9 of the source document. The titles and section headings in the output correspond to the structure of the original minutes. No fabricated body text or invented sections were identified.

All contact information present in the output, specifically the phone numbers for participants (352-672-4039 and 919-869-4013) found in the guest list on page 2, matches the source PDF. No fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs were found.

All major sections of the document, including the roll call tables (pages 1-2), the detailed minutes (pages 3-9), and the final vote roster (page 10), have corresponding representations in the JSON output. While the final table is truncated (see C4), the page/section itself is not entirely missing.

The table 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' at element index 18 corresponds to the vote roster on page 10 of the source. The source table contains 16 data rows (one for each board member). The output JSON only contains one data row (David Bone), omitting the remaining 15 members. This is a material omission of approximately 94% of the table's data rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on page 10 contains 16 data rows for board members Tommy Cole, Greg Coltrain, Brian Drew, Bo Ferguson, Greg Foster, Chuck Greene, J.D. Hartman, Melanie Jones, Lee Kerlin, Jeff Ledford, John Moore, Jude O'Sullivan, Jeff Shipp, Earl Struble, Jim Weaver, and Donna Wright.
checks.b_evidence_output: The data rows in the table at element index 18 only include: { "cell_type": "data", "content": [ { "text": "David Bone..." } ], ... }
checks.b_evidence_location: NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE, Page 10, element index 18

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The meeting minutes text from pages 3 through 9 has been transcribed into several paragraph elements (8 through 16). A spot-check of key financial figures and specific project updates (e.g., Wilkes County migration, cumulative costs of eligibility requests) shows that the substantive content of these sections is present without significant omissions.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output contains some minor OCR artifacts such as spelling errors in the guest table ('Misson' for 'Mission', 'Comminucations' for 'Communications') and a missing closing parenthesis in the Board Members table ('VoIP' for 'VoIP)'). However, as the source is a scanned document, these are considered acceptable minor OCR differences that do not materially alter the information conveyed to a government employee.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains repeated phrases such as 'Current migration demographics include 54 hosted Vesta, 49 hosted Viper, and 22 ESInet-Only.' within the Technology Committee Report. However, cross-referencing with page 8 of the source document reveals that this repetition exists in the original minutes. No duplication was introduced by the pipeline.

The output uses H2 for major sections like 'Roll Call' and 'Roll Call Votes'. While many sub-headings (1., 2., 3., etc.) are merged into paragraph text, the heading levels used do not skip (e.g., H1 to H3). This is considered an acceptable adjustment for accessibility normalization.

The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' table on page 10 is severely truncated. The source table lists 16 board members and staff, providing voting records for each. The output JSON only contains a single data row (David Bone), omitting approximately 15 rows of data.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on page 10 contains rows for all board members (David Bone, Tommy Cole, Greg Coltrain, Brian Drew, Bo Ferguson, Greg Foster, Chuck Greene, J.D. Hartman, Melanie Jones, Lee Kerlin, Jeff Ledford, John Moore, Jude O'Sullivan, Jeff Shipp, Earl Struble, Jim Weaver, Donna Wright).
checks.b_evidence_output: The table object at element index 18 only contains two rows: one header row and one data row for 'David Bone'.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order follows the source document page-by-page and section-by-section. No major reordering was detected.

The document contains numerous structured lists (numbered items 1-14, lettered subsections a-j, and bulleted progress reports). In the output, these lists are entirely flattened into single, massive paragraph blocks. The semantic meaning and structural hierarchy of these lists are lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source pages 3-9 use distinct numbering (1., 2...) and lettering (a), b)...) for meeting items, as well as bullet points for grant updates.
checks.b_evidence_output: Content is merged into paragraph blocks such as element index 12, which combines items 1 through 5b into a single text block.

checks.a_analysis: Hyperlinks present in the source document (e.g., 'Live Status Map' on page 3 and 'SIP Admin' on page 8) are rendered as plain text in the output. While the text is accurate, the link functionality is missing. Per instructions, missing links are a minor structural defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3: 'Live Status Map' is a blue hyperlink. Page 8: 'SIP Admin' is a blue hyperlink.
checks.b_evidence_output: The words 'Live Status Map' and 'SIP Admin' appear in paragraph text without a corresponding URL or clickable markup.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The meeting header information is followed by the roll call tables for board members and staff, then guests. The narrative sections (items 1-14) are presented in a clean, single-column flow that matches the document's structure. The final 'Roster of Roll Call Votes' table is placed at the end of the document, just as in the PDF. The responsive layout of the HTML effectively preserves the logical flow of information.

All tables from the source PDF, including the 'Roll Call' tables and the complex 'Roster of Roll Call Votes' table, are correctly rendered in the HTML. The rows and columns are clearly defined with borders or appropriate spacing, ensuring that headers are distinct from body rows and content is properly aligned. Specifically, the 'Roster of Roll Call Votes' table successfully handles long vertical headers from the PDF by rendering them as vertical text, maintaining the readability of the vote tallies (Y, N/P, Excused) for each member.

The meaningful images from the source PDF, specifically the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logos, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output at both the beginning and end of the document. The large red watermarks ('APPROVED BY NC 911 BOARD...') have been omitted, which is an intentional and acceptable improvement for accessibility as they are decorative/non-content elements.

The HTML document was reviewed for visual duplication of body content. Each section, paragraph, and table appears only once in the main body. The presence of document titles in both the navigation/header area and the body is an expected accessibility feature and does not constitute content duplication.
